description_long: This object is a historically styled machine gun with a long, slender black metal body and a wooden fixed stock. The receiver and barrel assembly are detailed with visible mechanical components, including a prominent magazine well and a curved detachable box magazine. The handguard and fore-end are constructed from dark metal, with a front sight post and a rear sight mounted along the top. The trigger and pistol grip are ergonomically shaped, and the wooden stock features a smooth, slightly curved design. The overall finish is matte with some subtle textural variation, and the magazine is distinctly curved, suggesting a mid-20th-century design. The object is compact for a machine gun, with no visible mounting bipod or advanced attachments.
description: A compact, black metal machine gun with a curved box magazine and a smooth wooden fixed stock. It features detailed iron sights and an ergonomic pistol grip.
description_view_0: The machine gun is seen from the muzzle end, showing the narrow cylindrical barrel and the alignment of the front sight post.
description_view_1: A full side profile reveals the wooden stock, curved box magazine, and the detailed receiver with iron sights and trigger assembly.
description_view_2: Viewed from the rear, the wooden stock and the straight alignment of the barrel and sights are visible, with the magazine partially obscured.
description_view_3: The opposite side profile displays the same key features as view 1, with the magazine, stock, and iron sights clearly visible.
attribute_stock_design: fixed wooden stock with smooth, slightly curved design
attribute_iron_sight_configuration: fixed rear notch with front post
